rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA L. HUDNALL VS. HINES GS PROPERTIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Physical Examination In California
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday July 27, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DRYWALL CO.'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Physical Examination In California. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SURAJ RAJWANI VS. ROBERT LEATHERN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment Or, Alternatively, Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 27, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANT ROBERT LEATHERN's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Motion For Summary Adjudication. Defendant Robert Leathern's motion for summary judgment or alternatively summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Mr. Leathern fails to show that he is entitled to judgment as a matter of law in his favor as to any of the four causes of action alleged against him on any of the many grounds that he has asserted in his moving papers. Mr. Leathern fails to show that the parties had an oral agreement and the two-year statute of limitations applies. The October 16, 2013 e-mail sets forth Mr. Leathern's obligations and plaintiff Suraj Rajwani's promise to consent to the merger. The golden parachute can be implied from that agreement. Mr. Rajwani accepted the agreement by consenting to the merger and golden parachute. There is a triable issue whether the 3-year statute of limitations bars the fraud claim. Mr. Leathern informed Mr. Rajwani that he would not perform in November 2013. Mr. Leathern, however, ignores Mr. Rajwani's allegations and evidence regarding the parties' attempt to locate a nominee that would purchase the shares on Mr. Leathern's behalf. Paragraphs 33-38 of Mr. Rajwani's declaration create a triable issue whether he learned of the facts underlying the fraud claim in May 2014. There is a triable dispute whether the release bars Mr. Rajwani's claims. The release related to the merger and the release of Optimal, HoldCo and Brand Networks and its officers/directors. The agreement between the parties was a confidential side deal unrelated to that transaction. Mr. Leathern was not acting as an officer of the entities when he made the side deal. Mr. Leathern's text that "Yes but to be clear this is between you and I and not involving the company cool?" supports this finding. (Mr. Leathern's understanding of the parties' side agreement before a dispute arose is probative. (So. Cal. Edison Co. v. Sup. Crt. (1995) 37 Cal.App.4th 839, 851.) Mr. Lethern's consideration argument lacks merit. "[A]n option without consideration is not binding on either party until exercised; until then, the option " 'is simply a continuing offer which may be revoked at any time.'" (Steiner v. Thexton (2010) 48 Cal.4th 411, 420 (citation omitted)). Mr. Rajwani exercised the option before revocation. In addition, Mr. Rajwani's agreement to the golden parachute could constitute sufficient consideration. Mr. Leathern admitted that the golden parachute or "280G Consent" was an issue separate from the merger. In other words, the golden parachute approval could be viewed as distinct consideration. Mr. Leathern fails to show that Mr. Rajwani did not incur damage. The economic loss rule does not bar fraud claims. (Erlich v. Menezes (1999) 21 Cal.4th 543, 551-552.) Mr. Leathern fails to show a lack of justifiable reliance and Mr. Rajwani's forbearance could constitute reliance. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Rajwani is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES ANDREW DOEDEN ET AL VS. KLS MARTIN MANUFACTURING LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Quash Service For Lack Of Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 27, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT KARL LEIBINGER MEDIZINTECHNIK GMBH & CO. KG's Hearing On Motion To Quash Service For Lack Of Jurisdiction. Defendant Karl Leibinger Medizintechnik GMBH & Co. KG.'s ("KLM") motion to quash is continued November 15, 2018 to allow plaintiffs to take jurisdictional discovery. KLM correctly cites Felix v. Bomoro Kommanditgesellschaft (1987) 196 Cal.App.3d 106, 114 for the proposition that the mere foreseeability that a product may wind up in a forum due to the stream of commerce is not enough for jurisdiction. (See also Dow Chemical Canada ULC v. Sup.Ct. (Fandino) (2011) 202 Cal.App.4th 170, 179.) Plaintiffs presently do not show that KLM-as opposed to KLS-has sufficient minimum contacts to warrant imposition of California jurisdiction over it. The undisputed evidence shows that KLM is a German entity and it has no place of business, officers, employees, bank accounts or advertisements in California. (Lunny Dec. pars. 8-9). The declaration of Dr. Jaffurs fails to demonstrate contacts to support jurisdiction as his interactions were with KLS and his "understanding" regarding how information is transferred to KLM is vague. Plaintiffs may conduct jurisdictional discovery regarding the relationship between KLS and KLM including the amount of control KLM has exerted over KLS. Plaintiffs may depose KLM's PMK(s) for no more than 8 hours, serve no more than 20 special interrogatories, and serve no more than 20 requests for production of documents including requests included in deposition notices. Supplemental opposition papers must be filed and served no later than November 1, 2018 and supplemental reply papers must be filed and served no later than November 8, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: STANFORD-STARTX FUND, LLC ET AL VS. MEDWHAT.COM INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 27, 2018, Line 10, DEFENDANT ARTURO DEVESA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Arturo Devesa's demurrer to the first through fifth causes of action in the complaint is off calendar as to the first cause of action and overruled as to the second through fifth causes of action. The first cause of action is not alleged against Mr. Devesa, so there is no basis for him to demurrer to that cause of action. The second through fifth causes of action seek to hold Mr. Devesa liable based on Mr. Devesa's own conduct and not on the basis of the alter ego doctrine, so Mr. Devesa's arguments on the grounds of the alter ego doctrine are immaterial. To the extent that the demurrer is based on lack of personal jurisdiction over Mr. Devesa, he waived the right to contest jurisdiction by filing an answer before filing this demurrer and, even if he didn't, he fails to develop any coherent argument why the court lacks personal jurisdiction over him. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Devesa is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: JUSTICE INVESTORS, LP ET AL VS. GEOFFREY M. PALERMO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Action Pending Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 27, 2018, Line 13, DEFENDANT GEOFFREY PALERMO, GMP MANAGEMENT, INC.'s Motion To Stay Action Pending Arbitration. Defendants Geoffrey Palermo and GMP Management, Inc.'s motion to stay action pending arbitration is granted. Defendants' motion for a stay is treated as the functional equivalent of a motion to compel arbitration. All thirteen claims alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Justice Investors, LP and Portsmouth Square, Inc. are encompassed by the broad scope of the arbitration clause because they are rooted in the business relationship between the parties and concern the management of the Hotel. (EFund Capital Partners v. Pless (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 1311, 1322 ("arbitration should be upheld 'unless it can be said with assurance that an arbitration clause is not susceptible to an interpretation covering the asserted dispute" and finding conversion and breach of fiduciary duty to be arbitrable claims having their roots in strategic relations contractual agreement between the parties)). Arbitration of the claims may be required by Mr. Palermo and required of Portsmouth Square, even though they are not signatories to the MSA, because "A nonsignatory plaintiff can be compelled to arbitrate a claim even against a nonsignatory defendant, when the claim is itself based on, or inextricably intertwined with, the contract containing the arbitration clause." (JSM Tuscany, LLC v. Superior Court (2011) 193 Cal. App. 4th 1222, 1241). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)
